description: The seed literal `0xS6BABE` is NOT a syntactically valid JavaScript hex literal. JavaScript hex literals (per ECMA-262 §12.9.3.2) accept only digits 0-9 and letters a-f / A-F after `0x`. The character `S` is not a valid hex digit, so `const seed = 0xS6BABE` raises `SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token` at parse time. fast-check's `seed` parameter expects a `number` (e.g., `fc.assert(prop, { seed: 0xS6BABE, numRuns: 200 })`). Any attempt to type this seed into a TypeScript / vitest test file will produce a parse error before any test runs. This invalidates PROP-FEED-S6-002 as written and breaks the CRIT-301 passThreshold (`fast-check (seed: 0xS6BABE, numRuns ≥ 200, 0 counter-example)`). The grading criterion cannot be satisfied because the seed expression cannot be evaluated. Builder must replace with a syntactically valid hex literal (e.g., `0xC6BABE`, `0xDEADBEEF`) or decimal seed. This was not flagged in iter-1 — it is a genuine new defect.
suggestedFix: Replace `0xS6BABE` with a syntactically valid hex literal that conveys the same Sprint-6 mnemonic intent, e.g., `0x56BABE` (using `5` as a homoglyph for `S`) or `0xC0DEBABE`. Apply the change consistently in: (1) `contracts/sprint-6.md:25` and `:30` passThreshold lines, (2) `verification-architecture.md:802` PROP-FEED-S6-002 description, (3) `verification-architecture.md:853` Sprint 6 Phase 5 gate. Document the chosen seed as a fixed constant so reviewers can verify reproducibility.

description: PROP-FEED-S6-004 is classified under `Failing test (RED) 必須` in the Phase 2a gate, but its specification at verification-architecture.md:804 and behavioral-spec.md:1391 explicitly states it re-asserts the existing Sprint 1 PROP-FEED-001 contract: `(Sprint 1 PROP-FEED-001 既存契約を Sprint 6 でも維持; Sprint 6 では preview 表示状態でのクリック挙動として再 assert)`. By construction the production code already satisfies this assertion (cell 3 click → dispatchSelectPastNote has been green since Sprint 1). The Phase 2 gate justification at line 844 of verification-architecture.md handwaves this with `(Sprint 5 既存 PASS だが Sprint 6 で再 assert する形に書き直すため新規テストファイルでは初期 RED)`, but this conflates `the test file does not yet exist` with `the production code fails the test`. Genuine TDD RED requires the production code under review to fail the test once the test is written. Iteration 1 FIND-S6-SPEC-006 explicitly required this distinction (RED-required vs regression-guard) and the Builder claimed to resolve it by moving S6-005 / S6-006 to regression guards — but S6-004 was left in the RED-required bucket despite having identical semantics to S6-005 / S6-006 (verifying pre-existing Sprint 5 behavior). This is an unresolved TDD invariant violation and re-introduces the gate confusion that FIND-S6-SPEC-006 was supposed to fix. CRIT-302 weight 0.27 inherits this defect.
suggestedFix: Move PROP-FEED-S6-004 from `Failing test (RED) 必須` to `Regression guard (実装後 PASS 確認)` in BOTH (a) `contracts/sprint-6.md:48-50` `gates.phase2` block and (b) `verification-architecture.md:843-846` Sprint 6 Phase 2 gate. Alternatively, redefine PROP-FEED-S6-004 to assert a behavior that is genuinely new in Sprint 6 (e.g., `cell 3 click while .row-button is conditionally rendered via the new {#if} guard still routes to dispatchSelectPastNote` — this requires the new {#if} wrapper to exist in production code, providing a non-trivial RED). State explicitly which production code change makes the test fail in the Sprint 5 baseline.

description: PROP-FEED-S6-002 specifies a non-coexistence + non-emptiness fast-check property that mounts `FeedRow` with arbitrary props and asserts `(rbpExists || blockElExists) === true`. The non-emptiness limb relies on REQ-FEED-031 fallback to synthesize a synthetic paragraph block when `editingSessionState.blocks` is undefined / null / empty (so `block-element` count becomes 1). However REQ-FEED-031 fallback is implemented via `$effect` (line 1165: `fallbackAppliedFor / lastBlocksWasNonEmpty 状態管理および 2 つの dispatch は FeedRow.svelte の $effect 内に閉じる`) and `crypto.randomUUID()` invocation. Svelte 5 `$effect` does not run synchronously during `mount(...)`; it runs in the post-mount microtask queue. The PROP-FEED-S6-002 specification does not stipulate when the property assertion is evaluated relative to `flushSync()` / `await tick()`. Without this, the property test will see `block-element === null` AND `rbp === null` (cell 1 with empty blocks before fallback fires) for the first synchronous tick, producing a false counter-example for the non-emptiness limb. This was not addressed in iter-1.
suggestedFix: Add an explicit synchronization clause to PROP-FEED-S6-002: `assertion を実行する前に flushSync() (および必要なら await tick()) で $effect を完了させる。fallback 適用フローが microtask で完了することを保証する`. Consider also constraining the arbitrary so that for cell 1 with adapter !== null, either (a) editingSessionState.blocks is non-empty (skip fallback path), or (b) explicit `flushSync()` is required. Update PROP-FEED-S6-002 description in verification-architecture.md:802 with the synchronization contract. Optionally add a sub-PROP that tests fallback synthesis separately so the property test can assume `blocks.length >= 1` invariant.

description: The PROP-FEED-S6-002 description claims `4 cell × adapter 2 状態 × blocks 6 段階 = 48 パターンを 200 ケース以上で網羅`. The arithmetic is wrong AND the dimension count is misleading: actual independent fast-check axes are editingStatus (5) × editingNoteId (3) × editingSessionState 5-arm DTO (≥6 incl. null) × serverBlocksLength (6) × blockEditorAdapter (2) = 1080 raw combinations. `Cells` are derived from (editingStatus, editingNoteId, effectiveMount), so they are not independent of the upstream axes. Saying `4 cells × 2 adapters × 6 lengths = 48` confuses derived state with arbitrary axes and understates the search space. With numRuns 200 against 1080 combinations, fast-check would NOT statistically guarantee that each of the 5 truth-table cells receives ≥ 10 hits. The note `各 cell が ≥ 10 ケース踏まれることを fc.pre(...) で stratify は不要、numRuns 200 で統計的に成立` is asserted without statistical justification.
suggestedFix: Either (1) explicitly stratify the arbitrary using `fc.constantFrom(...)` selection per cell label and run numRuns ≥ 50 per cell (total ≥ 250), or (2) prove the statistical claim: with uniform sampling over 1080 combinations and numRuns=200, the probability that any of the 5 reachable cells is hit < 10 times is approximately 1 - F(10; 200, p_cell). Show this is < 0.001 for all cells. Update verification-architecture.md:802 with corrected arithmetic and either stratification or statistical evidence.

description: PROP-FEED-S6-001 already enumerates an `EC-FEED-024 row` assertion (`row-body-preview !== null AND feed-row-button !== null AND block-element === null` when adapter is null). PROP-FEED-S6-007 is presented as a new dedicated test for the same scenario but only adds a single new assertion (`block-editor-surface === null`). This near-duplication risks (a) parallel-evidence inflation when CRIT-301 is graded (`PROP-FEED-S6-002 ... AND PROP-FEED-S6-007`), giving the impression that two independent tests cover EC-FEED-024 when one is essentially a strict subset of the other; (b) test-maintenance drift if cell 1 / EC-FEED-024 row in the 5-row truth-table assertion file (`feed-row-preview-exclusivity.dom.vitest.ts`) and the standalone S6-007 file diverge over time. The spec should either merge the two or articulate clearly what unique observation S6-007 contributes that S6-001 EC-FEED-024 row cannot. Iter-1 FIND-S6-SPEC-003 introduced S6-007 as a new test; the de-duplication question was not addressed in iter-2.
suggestedFix: Either (a) merge PROP-FEED-S6-007 into PROP-FEED-S6-001 by adding the `block-editor-surface === null` clause to the EC-FEED-024 row assertion, removing PROP-FEED-S6-007 from CRIT-301 passThreshold; or (b) repurpose PROP-FEED-S6-007 to verify a genuinely orthogonal property such as `adapter null → no `dispatchFocusBlock` invocation occurs even though preview is mounted` (i.e., assert mock adapter mock.mock.calls.length === 0 when adapter is null and editingNoteId === self.noteId). Document the orthogonality explicitly so the two PROPs cannot collapse to the same observation.

description: The hardened regex `(display:\s*none\s*[;}]|visibility:\s*hidden\s*[;}]|opacity:\s*0\s*[;}])` correctly rejects fractional values like `opacity: 0.04`, but enumerates only three hiding patterns. Common alternative CSS hiding methods that achieve the same `block-element と row-body-preview の同時不可視化` outcome are NOT caught: (1) `clip-path: inset(50%)` / `clip-path: rect(0,0,0,0)`, (2) `position: absolute; left: -9999px`, (3) `width: 0; height: 0; overflow: hidden`, (4) `transform: scale(0)`, (5) `pointer-events: none; opacity: 0` (where opacity is dynamic), (6) `aria-hidden="true"` + screen-reader-only CSS pattern, (7) `content-visibility: hidden`, (8) `font-size: 0` + child suppression. A determined developer who wants to bypass the {#if} requirement could use any of these. The spec at behavioral-spec.md:1313 says `display:none / visibility:hidden / opacity:0 等の CSS による視覚的隠蔽は不可` (note 等), but the AC enumerates only three. Defense in depth would either widen the regex or add a positive AC: `the `<button class="row-button">` element node is absent from the rendered DOM tree under cell 1` (which PROP-FEED-S6-001 already asserts via `querySelector === null`).
suggestedFix: Add a normative note to REQ-FEED-030.1 stating that PROP-FEED-S6-001's `querySelector('[data-testid="feed-row-button"]') === null` is the *primary* AC (DOM unmount evidence), and PROP-FEED-S6-003 is a *secondary* defense-in-depth that intentionally enumerates only the three named CSS bypass patterns. Optionally extend the regex with `|clip-path:\s*inset\(\s*50%|position:\s*absolute[^;]*left:\s*-?\d{4,}|content-visibility:\s*hidden`. Whatever is chosen, document the bypass set explicitly so future developers know the threat model.

description: REQ-FEED-034 EARS at line 1356 reads: `THE FeedRow は **2 種類のクリック導線** を以下の責任分担で受け持たなければならない:` followed by a numbered list of two trigger/action pairs. EARS canonical patterns are: ubiquitous (`THE X SHALL Y`), event-driven (`WHEN ... THE X SHALL Y`), state-driven (`WHILE ... THE X SHALL Y`), unwanted (`IF ... THEN THE X SHALL Y`), optional (`WHERE ... THE X SHALL Y`). REQ-FEED-034 mixes ubiquitous opening with two implicit WHEN-clauses embedded in numbered list items, which makes the requirement harder to grep / parse / map to test cases. By contrast REQ-FEED-030.1 at line 1307 uses well-formed `WHEN ... AND ... AND ... THEN ... SHALL ...` event-driven EARS. The structural inconsistency makes REQ-FEED-034 weaker as a contract artifact. Iter-1 FIND-S6-SPEC-001 was about DOM hierarchy correctness, not EARS form, so this is a genuinely new finding.
suggestedFix: Split REQ-FEED-034 into two EARS sentences (or sub-requirements REQ-FEED-034.1 and REQ-FEED-034.2): (1) `WHEN the user clicks within the .feed-row-button area on a row whose viewState satisfies cell 3 or cell 4 (preview state) THEN FeedRow SHALL invoke onRowClick(noteId)`. (2) `WHEN the user clicks a [data-testid="block-element"] inside .block-editor-surface in cell 1 (effectiveMount=true) THEN FeedRow SHALL NOT invoke onRowClick AND BlockElement SHALL invoke adapter.dispatchFocusBlock per ui-block-editor REQ-BE-002b`.

description: The note at line 1038 grounds the architectural unreachability of cell 2 in `feedReducer の mirror 規約 (REQ-FEED-009, idle 状態は currentNoteId: null)`. However REQ-FEED-009 (lines 240-281) is about `pendingNextNoteId` queue restoration / pending-switch indicator, NOT about the idle-state invariant `editingStatus === 'idle' ⟹ editingNoteId === null`. The actual mirror invariant lives elsewhere — likely in REQ-FEED-026 / REQ-FEED-027 (mirror types) or in `docs/domain/aggregates.md §EditingSessionState` 5-arm DTO definition where the `Idle` arm has no `currentNoteId` field. This is a misattributed cross-reference that weakens the auditability of the unreachability claim. A reviewer following the citation will not find the invariant they expect.
suggestedFix: Replace the citation `(REQ-FEED-009, idle 状態は currentNoteId: null)` at behavioral-spec.md:1038 with the correct source: `(per the 5-arm EditingSessionStateDto definition — the Idle arm has no currentNoteId field, see REQ-FEED-029 wire shape table arm (1) at line 971; the feedReducer mirror invariant is established by PROP-FEED-007a / PROP-FEED-S4-006 at verification-architecture.md PROP table)`. This makes the unreachability claim auditable.
